title: Update link to Commands documentation
body: The previous link pointed to the Markdown file, but the links are broken on that page. This pull request points to the actual documentation.

comments.body:
Hi @srp33. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
@srp33 our ci requires that submits are signed.  Please do `git commit --amend -s` and then repush.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *srp33*, *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@baude @mheon  @rhatdan.  The change that @srp33 is proposing is to change the commands link on the readme from our commands.md page to the podman.readthedocs.io.  The only downside to that is that the commands.md not only had links to the docs, but also links to demos of the commands.  Granted we're lagging way behind in the demo's bit of that on that page.  

Is there a way to add demo links to the readthedocs?  I'm guessing not.  I'm thinking we perhaps keep this change and move commands.md to command_demos.md or something (in a separate pr) and work those from there?
Yes lets setup a different page for demos.
@srp33 you need to run make validate ... it will identify some issue that needs correction for the tests to pass.

I apologize, but I'm not well-versed in doing pull requests. I cloned the repository, but I'm not sure what commands to run after that. (Or if it's easier for you, you can delete this pull request and do it on your own.)

@srp33 i took a look, it just needs a signature ... do this:

`git commit --amend -s` and then repush your pr.

I cloned the repository and ran that command. Then I tried git push, but it
said I don't have access. Sorry for these dumb questions.

You should have cloned the REPO on the github web site, Then clone your own REPO, and push the change up there.
Once you have pushed to your own REPO you can go to the web site and open a PR to the upstream to make the change.
Replacing this PR with my own, to make life easier on @srp33.  Thanks alot for the PR.
